concept: Lock / Mechanism Holding a Closure Secure
description: A mechanism that holds a door, lid, gate, or other closure until the matching means of release is used. Release may come from a key, latch, code, or another stated design. 'tapu' (close) is the action of shutting something; 'kirato' is the device that keeps the closure in place.
sound_symbolism: Crisp 'ki' opens into the stressed tongue roll and broad vowel of 'ra'. The opening is 'kira' (key) heard intact. Final 'to' meets the teeth in a clean stop and rounds into a firm low ending.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia roe kira ha kirato phae.' (I open this lock with a key.) The thing secured and the person controlling access remain separate relations. A source term may identify an exact lock design.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A chosen lock protects a boundary; another clause still says who holds the key and whose entry is affected.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wooden latches and fitted metal locks made privacy a matter of household craft long before standardized hardware.

semantic_domains.creation: a fitted mechanism for holding a closure
semantic_domains.physical: a device released by a matching key or method
semantic_domains.spatial: control at a door, lid, or gate
